level_signals: 8+ years of experience in Product Management, Data Product Leadership, or related roles
Compensation range $120,000–$202,500 consistent with senior IC at large financial institution
Responsibility for influencing senior, matrixed stakeholders and driving enterprise-level strategy

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in Product Management, Data Product Leadership, or related roles
Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred
Strong product thinking and strategic mindset, with the ability to articulate vision, trade‑offs, and long‑term value
Proven experience influencing senior, matrixed stakeholders in complex, regulated environments
Deep understanding of enterprise data products, client data domains, and platform‑based operating models
Ability to connect data and analytics capabilities to concrete business outcomes
Excellent executive communication, storytelling, and roadmap framing skills

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred
Experience building or scaling Client 360 / Customer 360 or enterprise client intelligence platforms preferred
Familiarity with modern data platforms, analytics ecosystems, and lakehouse architectures preferred
Exposure to data governance, privacy, entitlements, and AI‑enabled insights a plus
Experience operating at the intersection of business strategy, data, and technology in a highly governed financial services environment is strongly preferred.

scope.surface: Client 360 — enterprise client intelligence platform serving as the firm's golden source of client, revenue, pipeline, service, and relationship data
scope.business_model: internal enterprise platform (B2B internal), financial services
primary_metrics_inferred: Linkage of platform capabilities to commercial outcomes
core_responsibilities: Shape the end-to-end product vision and strategy for Client 360
Develop and maintain a forward-looking product roadmap balancing near-term needs and long-term scalability
Translate business goals into clear product outcomes, capability themes, and value-driven priorities
Act as the primary product partner to senior stakeholders across Commercial, Operations, Technology, Analytics, and Data Governance
Shape the enterprise client data model, domain strategy, and semantic consistency
Define success metrics for Client 360 including adoption, data confidence, insight usage, and business outcome linkage
Guide evolution of Client 360 to support advanced analytics, AI/GenAI use cases, and self-service consumption models

green_flags: Clear product ownership and platform scope described in detail
Strategic, outcome-oriented role with enterprise-wide impact
Part of a foundational technology function critical to firm-wide scalability
red_flags: No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization — unclear for non-US candidates
Location type not explicitly stated as hybrid or remote; likely onsite in Boston based on listing but not confirmed in body
Wide salary range ($120K–$202.5K) may indicate significant uncertainty in level or negotiation latitude
VP title but role appears to be individual contributor — may cause confusion for candidates expecting people management
No explicit statement about team size or direct reports

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level inferred from VP title in role name, comp range, and years requirement
seniority.manages_people inferred as false — no mention of direct reports in JD
location.type inferred as onsite from city listing in URL and Massachusetts job disclosure; body does not explicitly state hybrid/remote policy
scope.business_model inferred from product description as internal enterprise platform within financial services
scope.primary_metrics_inferred derived from responsibilities; not enumerated as metrics in JD
compensation.location_pay_differential inferred from explicit statement 'If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ'
ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated in body (hybrid, remote, or onsite). URL references Boston, Massachusetts and the compensation disclaimer references a 'primary location', suggesting other locations may be possible but none are named. Treated as onsite for the primary location.

The financial services environment requirement is labeled 'strongly preferred' in the Additional Requirements section rather than required — could be interpreted as hard or soft; classified as soft (nice_to_have) given the language.
Role title contains 'VP' which at financial institutions often denotes a seniority band rather than people management; no direct reports mentioned — treated as senior IC.
